checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas calculation safe from integer overflow?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test with maximum possible base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length. 2. Calculate intermediate values (complexity * iterations). 3. Verify no overflow in 64-bit, 128-bit, or 256-bit arithmetic. 4. Check client source for appropriate integer types. 5. Test near-overflow values to trigger potential wrap-around.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0036, Trust Boundary: tb-006, STRIDE-EoP-tb-006

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are EIP-7825 and EIP-7883 activated simultaneously at Fusaka fork?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Check fork configuration for activation block/timestamp. 2. Verify EIP-7825 and EIP-7883 have identical activation. 3. Compare activation across all client implementations. 4. Test behavior at fork boundary (block N-1, N, N+1). 5. Any activation difference = consensus split risk.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0037, Trust Boundary: tb-007, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-007. Requires further investigation due to conditional scope.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do MEV-Boost relays validate gas cap for submitted blocks?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with oversized transaction to MEV-Boost relay. 2. Verify relay rejects the block. 3. Test with multiple relay implementations. 4. Verify proposer also rejects if relay somehow passes it. 5. Test end-to-end: malicious builder -> relay -> proposer.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0039, Trust Boundary: tb-008, STRIDE-Spoofing-tb-008

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch private channel txpool bypasses?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Simulate Flashbots Protect-style private channel. 2. Submit oversized transaction through private channel. 3. Include in block and submit to validators. 4. Verify block validation catches violation. 5. Confirm no bypass path exists for private transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0040, Trust Boundary: tb-008, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-008

checklist_item.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Is ceiling division used in multiplication_complexity for words?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test max_length=33: words = ceil(33/8) = 5 (not floor = 4). 2. Test max_length=39: words = ceil(39/8) = 5. 3. Test max_length=40: words = ceil(40/8) = 5. 4. Test max_length=41: words = ceil(41/8) = 6. 5. Verify ceiling, not floor division throughout.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0042, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Pre-condition for q_large -> q_compute

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for small non-zero exponents?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test exponent=1 (bit_length=1): iteration_count = 0, but max(0,1) = 1. 2. Test exponent=2 (bit_length=2): iteration_count = 1. 3. Test exponent=128 (bit_length=8): iteration_count = 7. 4. Test exponent=255 (bit_length=8): iteration_count = 7. 5. Verify bit_length computation is correct.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0043, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_nonzero -> q_final (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is bit_length of top 32 bytes computed correctly for large exponents?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test exponent with leading zeros in top 32 bytes. 2. Verify bit_length only counts significant bits. 3. Test top_32_bytes = 0x00...01 (bit_length = 1). 4. Test top_32_bytes = 0x80...00 (bit_length = 256). 5. Verify correct extraction and bit counting.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0044, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_large -> q_compute_large (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist_item.title: Verify Invariant: Is ModExp gas computation using only integer arithmetic (no floating-point)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Audit client source code for ModExp gas calculation. 2. Verify no float/double types used. 3. Check all intermediate values are integer. 4. Verify floor() is on integer result, not float conversion. 5. Test on different hardware platforms for determinism.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0045, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Consensus-critical

checklist_item.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Is gas cap check on raw tx.gasLimit, not derived values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Audit txpool code for gas cap check location. 2. Verify check is on raw field, not after any transformation. 3. Test with gasLimit values near 2^32 boundary. 4. Test with gasLimit values near 2^64 boundary. 5. Verify no truncation before comparison.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0047, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State: q_receive -> q_check (SG-001)

checklist_item.title: Verify Invariant: Is ModExp gas cost monotonically non-decreasing with input size?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. For base_length = 1 to 1000, verify gas(n) <= gas(n+1). 2. For modulus_length = 1 to 1000, verify monotonicity. 3. For exponent_length = 1 to 1000, verify monotonicity. 4. For exponent value increasing, verify monotonicity. 5. Any decrease indicates formula bug.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0049, Trust Boundary: tb-005

checklist_item.title: Verify Invariant: Is EIP-7883 gas cost >= EIP-2565 gas cost for all inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create comprehensive test vector of ModExp inputs. 2. Compute EIP-2565 gas for each input. 3. Compute EIP-7883 gas for each input. 4. Verify EIP-7883 >= EIP-2565 for all cases. 5. Any regression indicates implementation bug.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0050, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ECDSA signature recovery correctly deriving sender addresses?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review recover_sender implementation for correct secp256k1_recover usage. 2. Verify keccak256 hash is correctly applied to recovered public key. 3. Test with malformed r/s/v values to ensure no sender address spoofing. 4. Attempt to craft a transaction that passes validation but returns wrong sender address. 5. Verify address derivation uses last 20 bytes of keccak256 hash.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0001. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. Critical severity - signature forgery would enable complete account impersonation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the ECDSA r-component range validation enforced?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify where r validation occurs in recover_sender. 2. Submit transaction with r == 0 and verify rejection. 3. Submit transaction with r == SECP256K1N and verify rejection. 4. Submit transaction with r == SECP256K1N - 1 and verify acceptance. 5. Check that rejection happens before any state access.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0002. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. Pre-condition requiring 0 < r < SECP256K1N.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-2 low-s normalization enforced?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with s == 0 and verify rejection. 2. Submit transaction with s > SECP256K1N / 2 and verify rejection. 3. Submit transaction with s == SECP256K1N / 2 and verify acceptance. 4. Attempt transaction malleability by flipping s to SECP256K1N - s. 5. Verify both transaction variants cannot coexist in mempool.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0003. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. High-s enables tx malleability per EIP-2.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are transaction type signing hashes non-colliding?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all transaction types (legacy, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04). 2. Verify each type has unique prefix bytes. 3. Attempt to find field values that produce colliding signing hashes across types. 4. Verify type prefixes do not overlap with valid RLP first bytes for legacy. 5. Check that a signature from one type cannot be reused for another type.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0004. Boundary: tb-009. Cross-type signature confusion would change transaction semantics.

checklist_item.title: Verify Conditional Scope: Pre-EIP-155 cross-chain replay vulnerability awareness
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify that v == 27 or v == 28 indicates pre-EIP-155 transaction. 2. Check if client defaults to pre-155 signing under any circumstances. 3. Verify warnings are displayed when handling pre-155 transactions. 4. Document any Fusaka-specific changes to pre-155 behavior. 5. Check that pre-155 transactions are still valid but flagged appropriately.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0005. CONDITIONAL SCOPE - Known design limitation. Only in-scope if Fusaka changes alter pre-155 behavior.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does snapshot mechanism provide true deep-copy isolation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review copy_trie implementation for deep vs shallow copy. 2. Create nested snapshots via contract calls. 3. Mutate state in inner call and verify outer snapshot remains unaffected. 4. Test rollback_transaction restores exact state from begin_transaction. 5. Check both _main_trie and _storage_tries are properly copied.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0006. Boundary: tb-003. Shallow copy would break transaction atomicity.

checklist_item.title: Is the snapshot count invariant maintained after begin_transaction?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Record len(state._snapshots) before begin_transaction. 2. Call begin_transaction and verify len(state._snapshots) increased by exactly 1. 3. Test nested begin_transaction calls maintain correct count. 4. Verify no snapshot count changes occur from concurrent operations. 5. Test edge cases with maximum nesting depth.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0007. Post-condition ensuring snapshot lifecycle integrity.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does commit_transaction correctly decrement snapshot count?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Record len(state._snapshots) before commit_transaction. 2. Call commit_transaction and verify len decreased by exactly 1. 3. Verify transient_storage._snapshots also decreases by 1. 4. Test complex call patterns that exercise commit under adversarial conditions. 5. Check for off-by-one errors in snapshot popping.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0008. Boundary: tb-003. Incorrect snapshot management could persist reverted state.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is created_accounts cleared only at outermost snapshot pop?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create nested transactions with account creation in inner call. 2. Commit inner transaction and verify created_accounts NOT cleared. 3. Commit outermost transaction and verify created_accounts IS cleared. 4. Test gas refund calculations depend on correct created_accounts state. 5. Verify EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT eligibility is correctly tracked.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0009. Boundary: tb-003. Affects EIP-2200 gas accounting and EIP-6780 constraints.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does rollback_transaction restore exact snapshot state?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Record complete state at begin_transaction. 2. Make multiple state modifications. 3. Trigger rollback_transaction via REVERT or out-of-gas. 4. Compare post-rollback state with recorded pre-modification state. 5. Verify both _main_trie and _storage_tries are identical to snapshot.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0010. Boundary: tb-003. Incomplete rollback breaks EVM atomicity guarantee.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transient storage correctly restored after rollback?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Set transient storage values before sub-call. 2. Modify transient storage in sub-call. 3. Trigger REVERT in sub-call. 4. Verify transient storage values match pre-sub-call state. 5. Test re-entrancy guard patterns using TSTORE-based locks.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0011. Boundary: tb-003. Transient storage desync could break re-entrancy guards (EIP-1153).

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are persistent and transient snapshot stacks synchronized?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify len(state._snapshots) == len(transient_storage._snapshots) at all times. 2. Test commit_transaction pops from both stacks atomically. 3. Test rollback_transaction pops from both stacks atomically. 4. Simulate exception during commit/rollback and verify no desync. 5. Check recovery behavior if stacks become desynchronized.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0012. Boundary: tb-003. Stack desync causes wrong rollback restoration.

checklist_item.title: Is get_account guaranteed to never return None?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call get_account for nonexistent addresses and verify EMPTY_ACCOUNT returned. 2. Verify get_account never returns None under any input. 3. Test with addresses that have been destroyed. 4. Test with addresses at state trie boundaries. 5. Verify downstream functions handle EMPTY_ACCOUNT correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0013. Invariant - None return would cause NoneType errors in state operations.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_account(None) preserve storage?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create account with storage. 2. Call set_account(addr, None). 3. Verify account removed from _main_trie. 4. Verify storage trie still exists in _storage_tries. 5. Document any code paths that call set_account(None) directly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0014. Boundary: tb-005. Orphaned storage could be inherited by new accounts.
